introSection: Cobra is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at PowerPark in Alahärmä, Vaasa, Finland. The ride is a Boomerang installation, manufactured by Vekoma. Opened in 2005, it is the first Boomerang to use Vekoma's MK1211 open-sided trains. The ride was constructed by RCS GmbH.

sections.title: Ride Experience
sections.content: The ride starts as the train is pulled backwards from the station and up the first lift hill by a catch car. After being released, the train passes through the station. At this point, smoke is released, adding an effect to the ride. The train the enters a cobra roll element (referred to as a boomerang by the designers), and then travels through a vertical loop. After being pulled up the second lift hill, the train is released to head backwards through each inversion once more, making the total amount of inversions per ride six. Brakes slow the train down as is passes through the station backwards, and then comes to a complete stop in the station.

fullText:
Cobra is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at PowerPark in Alahärmä, Vaasa, Finland. The ride is a Boomerang installation, manufactured by Vekoma. Opened in 2005, it is the first Boomerang to use Vekoma's MK1211 open-sided trains. The ride was constructed by RCS GmbH.

This is a Boomerang clone.



The ride starts as the train is pulled backwards from the station and up the first lift hill by a catch car. After being released, the train passes through the station. At this point, smoke is released, adding an effect to the ride. The train the enters a cobra roll element (referred to as a boomerang by the designers), and then travels through a vertical loop. After being pulled up the second lift hill, the train is released to head backwards through each inversion once more, making the total amount of inversions per ride six. Brakes slow the train down as is passes through the station backwards, and then comes to a complete stop in the station.
